perturbation.original_text: Each Sponsor represents and warrants to each other Party that such Sponsor is bound by and has complied with, and agrees that such Sponsor shall continue to be bound by and comply with, the Equity Commitment Letter and that each other Sponsor (other than any Defaulting Sponsor) shall be entitled to enforce, or direct the enforcement of, such Equity Commitment Letter in accordance with this Agreement if and only if the Sponsors (other than any Defaulting Sponsor) have determined by unanimous written consent that the conditions of the Offer have been satisfied or to waive all such conditions remaining unsatisfied. No Party shall attempt to enforce any Equity Commitment Letter until the conditions set forth in this Section have been satisfied. Purchaser shall have no right to enforce any Equity Commitment Letter unless and until directed to do so by the unanimous written consent of the Sponsors (other than any Defaulting Sponsor).
perturbation.changed_text: Each Sponsor represents and warrants to each other Party that such Sponsor is bound by and has complied with, and agrees that such Sponsor shall continue to be bound by and comply with, the Equity Commitment Agreement and that each other Sponsor (other than any Defaulting Sponsor) shall be entitled to enforce, or direct the enforcement of, such Equity Commitment Agreement in accordance with this Agreement if and only if the Sponsors (other than any Defaulting Sponsor) have determined by unanimous written consent that the conditions of the Offer have been satisfied or to waive all such conditions remaining unsatisfied. No Party shall attempt to enforce any Equity Commitment Contract until the conditions set forth in this Section have been satisfied. Purchaser shall have no right to enforce any Equity Commitment Undertaking unless and until directed to do so by the unanimous written consent of the Sponsors (other than any Defaulting Sponsor).
perturbation.explanation: By using the terms "Equity Commitment Agreement", "Equity Commitment Contract" and "Equity Commitment Undertaking" interchangeably with the original term "Equity Commitment Letter" without defining them differently, creates an in-text contradiction and uncertainty as to which document is being referred to. The terms should be consistent throughout the document, and this variability introduces ambiguity in enforcement because each of those terms, can be interpreted as a different financial document, therefore it creates a problem with what each sponson is agreeing on.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Sponsor represents and warrants to each other Party that such Sponsor is bound by and has complied with, and agrees that such Sponsor shall continue to be bound by and comply with, the Equity Commitment Agreement and that each other Sponsor (other than any Defaulting Sponsor) shall be entitled to enforce, or direct the enforcement of, such Equity Commitment Agreement in accordance with this Agreement if and only if the Sponsors (other than any Defaulting Sponsor) have determined by unanimous written consent that the conditions of the Offer have been satisfied or to waive all such conditions remaining unsatisfied. No Party shall attempt to enforce any Equity Commitment Contract until the conditions set forth in this Section have been satisfied. Purchaser shall have no right to enforce any Equity Commitment Undertaking unless and until directed to do so by the unanimous written consent of the Sponsors (other than any Defaulting Sponsor).
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces the terms "Equity Commitment Agreement", "Equity Commitment Contract", and "Equity Commitment Undertaking" in place of the original term "Equity Commitment Letter". The contradicted text contains the same modifications, as the change text, indicating that these terms are being used interchangeably. As per the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' perturbation definition, using multiple terms interchangeably without defining them leads to conflicting obligations and uncertainty. This creates a direct conflict and ambiguity as it's unclear which specific document each Sponsor is bound by, impacting enforceability. The contradictory text exhibits the same issue.

perturbation.original_text: Each Sponsor that has complied with the Equity Commitment Letter and this Agreement ("Participating Sponsor") may terminate the participation in the Offer of any Sponsor that has not satisfied the funding requirements contemplated by the Equity Commitment Letter or otherwise failed to comply with the Equity Commitment Letter and this Agreement ("Defaulting Sponsor"); provided, however, that such termination shall not affect any right of any Participating Sponsor as against such Defaulting Sponsor with respect to such failure to fund or any such other non-compliance.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Sponsor that has complied with the Equity Commitment Letter and this Agreement ("Active Sponsor") may terminate the participation in the Offer of any Sponsor that has not satisfied the funding requirements contemplated by the Equity Commitment Letter or otherwise failed to comply with the Equity Commitment Letter and this Agreement ("Inactive Sponsor"); provided, however, that such termination shall not affect any right of any Active Sponsor as against such Inactive Sponsor with respect to such failure to fund or any such other non-compliance.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "Participating Sponsor" to "Active Sponsor" and "Defaulting Sponsor" to "Inactive Sponsor" without clarifying whether these terms are interchangeable or have distinct meanings, it introduces ambiguity and contradiction. This creates confusion when interpreting the rights and obligations of sponsors under the agreement, potentially affecting enforcement as it may lead to disputes over the definition of participation and default.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party shall use commercially reasonable efforts to negotiate and enter into at or prior to the Acceptance Time: (a) an amended and restated limited liability company agreement of Purchaser ("Purchaser LLC Agreement"); and (b) one or more definitive agreements with the Participating Sponsors (collectively, "Security Holder Agreements") providing for the unanimous consent of the Continuing Sponsors with respect to all decisions to be made by or on behalf of Purchaser with respect to any Offer Matter or the Company or any security issued or to be issued by the Company during the period on and from the Acceptance Date and until the time there ceases to be at least two Continuing Sponsors ("Interim Period"), including without limitation, any matter described in or contemplated by Section 1 hereof. For purposes hereof, "Continuing Sponsor" shall mean and refer to each Participating Sponsor that becomes a member of Purchaser and a party to the Purchaser LLC Agreement and Security Holder Agreements (collectively, the "Management-Ownership Agreements"), in each case, as long as such Sponsor continues to be a member of Purchaser and comply with the Management-Ownership Agreements.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Participating Sponsor" to "Active Sponsor" and "Defaulting Sponsor" to "Inactive Sponsor" introduces misaligned terminology, a form of in-text contradiction. The contradicted text refers to "Participating Sponsors" and "Continuing Sponsors", defining the latter as each "Participating Sponsor" that becomes a member of Purchaser. The change raises ambiguity about whether "Active Sponsor" and "Inactive Sponsor" are equivalent to "Participating Sponsor" and "Defaulting Sponsor", respectively. This contradiction creates uncertainty about which sponsors are bound by the Security Holder Agreements and the Purchaser LLC Agreement, potentially affecting the unanimous consent requirement for decisions made by Purchaser.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding anything herein or in any Management-Ownership Agreement to the contrary, each Continuing Sponsor may, at any time or from time to time in such Sponsor's sole discretion, cause Purchaser to distribute or otherwise transfer to such Sponsor all (but not less than all) of the amount of the Shares, if any, then owned by Purchaser equal to such Sponsor's pro rata share thereof (based on the Units then owned by all Sponsors) in redemption of or otherwise in exchange and as consideration for the Units then owned by such Sponsor.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding anything herein or in any Management-Ownership Agreement to the contrary, each Continuing Backer may, at any time or from time to time in such Backer's sole discretion, cause Purchaser to distribute or otherwise transfer to such Backer all (but not less than all) of the amount of the Shares, if any, then owned by Purchaser equal to such Backer's pro rata share thereof (based on the Units then owned by all contributors) in redemption of or otherwise in exchange and as consideration for the Units then owned by such Backer.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "Continuing Sponsor" to "Continuing Backer", as well as "Sponsors" to "contributors" the contract is now suffering from multiple term definitions, since it's not defined that all those words share the same legal meaning, creating therefore confusion, that will ultimately cause issues in a court of law, when enforcing said contract. Also, given that the definition for "Continuing Sponsor" already exists, further perpetuates this confusing in the text, since it's not clear what each term means and how they are being used.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party shall use commercially reasonable efforts to negotiate and enter into at or prior to the Acceptance Time: (a) an amended and restated limited liability company agreement of Purchaser ("Purchaser LLC Agreement"); and (b) one or more definitive agreements with the Participating Sponsors (collectively, "Security Holder Agreements") providing for the unanimous consent of the Continuing Sponsors with respect to all decisions to be made by or on behalf of Purchaser with respect to any Offer Matter or the Company or any security issued or to be issued by the Company during the period on and from the Acceptance Date and until the time there ceases to be at least two Continuing Sponsors ("Interim Period"), including without limitation, any matter described in or contemplated by Section 1 hereof. For purposes hereof, "Continuing Sponsor" shall mean and refer to each Participating Sponsor that becomes a member of Purchaser and a party to the Purchaser LLC Agreement and Security Holder Agreements (collectively, the "Management-Ownership Agreements"), in each case, as long as such Sponsor continues to be a member of Purchaser and comply with the Management-Ownership Agreements.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Continuing Sponsor" to "Continuing Backer" and "Sponsors" to "contributors" introduces misaligned terminology. The contradicted text defines "Continuing Sponsor" specifically in relation to the "Purchaser LLC Agreement" and "Security Holder Agreements". By using the terms "Continuing Backer" and "contributors" interchangeably without defining them or clarifying their relationship to the defined term "Continuing Sponsor", the contract creates ambiguity and a direct contradiction. This contradiction stems from the fact that the document now has terms that could be interpreted differently, making it unclear which entities are bound by the obligations and rights associated with the Continuing Sponsors, therefore creating a strong in-text contradiction, since it's not clear what each term means and how they are being used.
